summary: Implemented the M7 core action-loop UX recovery. The default first screen now exposes a core action loop and correctly prioritizes the District 1 obligation before appointment work; when the player intentionally opens the appointment flow, the same loop follows appointment preview, confirmation, and result states. The selected map district now has a direct obligation handling context, the task rail obligation drawer shows a concrete handling panel, and the district inspector routes to the same entry. Appointment preview now shows before/after, cost, benefit, risk, and will-change copy; confirmation produces durable accepted/submitted/rejected state, disables or relabels repeated confirmation, and makes success/failure visible beyond a small status sentence.

tests_run.result: PASS; TypeScript accepted the UI prop/state changes.
PASS; 1 file / 17 tests passed, including static core action loop and obligation entry assertions.

tests_run.command: pnpm exec playwright test tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts -g "appointment preview|M7 player guidance lite supports|M3 appointment player flow"
tests_run.result: PASS; 5 focused Chromium tests passed for appointment preview/confirm and existing appointment flow regressions.
PASS; Vite build completed with the existing chunk-size warning only.
PASS; web format, lint, typecheck, and test all passed.

PASS after Prettier write; format, lint, typecheck, and architecture checks passed.
PASS; architecture, deterministic canary, 43 repo test files / 353 tests, and web vitest passed.

decisions: Default first-screen phase now prioritizes selected-district obligations over appointment suggestions, so District 1 troop obligation is no longer hidden by appointment affordances.
Once the player opens the appointment drawer, the action loop intentionally follows appointment compare, preview, confirm, and result states until that flow resolves.

The task rail obligation drawer no longer uses Review obligations as the only ambiguous action; it shows an obligation handling panel and a separate Submit obligation support command path that still reuses existing command boundaries.
Appointment confirmation now changes visible state and relabels/blocks duplicate confirmation instead of leaving a normal-looking confirm button.
No authoritative simulation state, sim-core, protocol, save schema, content-source, Electron, final art, telemetry, server, multiplayer, or manual node battle behavior was changed.
risks: This remains a low-fidelity interaction recovery, not final strategic-map art direction or final Nobunaga-like presentation polish.

Submit obligation support currently uses the existing M4 start-march command path because M7 scope forbids new authoritative obligation mechanics; gameplay review should confirm the wording is acceptable as a minimal obligation handling abstraction.
The map still needs the next planned task M7-MAP-DECISION-SURFACE-SYNC-001 for broader map/inspector synchronization and strategic readability.
requested_action: Review the player-facing interaction recovery: default obligation priority, task rail/district/map obligation entry discoverability, appointment preview/confirm/result clarity, and phase/action loop readability. If accepted, route to independent QA; if not, request changes back to the same branch.
